title: Pronoun vagueness
description: Help clarify the sentence by keeping or changing existing pronouns.
topic_path: Indefinite pronouns, pronoun vagueness, and emphatic pronouns

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Rebecca told Lauren that Abby said __she__ was the best trombonist in school.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
"She" could refer to Rebecca, Lauren, or Abby. 

To make the sentence clear, we should replace "she" with a specific noun.
"The girl" or "that one" could still refer to any of the girls, so we should use the name of the particular girl, **"Lauren"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Carlos and Matt were strolling along the boardwalk when __he__ slipped on a banana peel.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "He" could refer to Carlos or to Matt. Which choice clarifies "he"?
"The boy" or "one" could still refer to either boy, so we should be more specific and say **"Carlos"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Pamela and Amy stopped at a gas station so __she__ could buy some snacks.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "She" could refer to Pamela or to Amy. Which choice clarifies "she"?
"The hungry one" and "that girl" could still refer to either girl, so we should be more specific and say **"Amy"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Roxana found Mei’s soccer ball in __her__ backyard.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "Her" could refer to Roxana or to Mei. Which choice clarifies "her"?
"That" and "the girl's" could still refer to either girl, so we should be more specific and say **"Roxana's"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Deirdre told Winifred she would read __her__ favorite book.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "Her" could refer to Deirdre or to Winifred. Which choice clarifies "her"?
"The" and "a" do not clarify whose favorite book is being referred to, so we should be more specific and say **"Winnifred's"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>When Diego approached the koala, __he__ became distressed.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "He" could refer to Diego or to the koala. Which choice clarifies "he"?
"One of them" and "the male" could still refer to either Diego or the koala, so we should be more specific and say **"the koala"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Although Leslie and Cherise both love peanuts, __she__ rarely has peanut butter in her house.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "She" could refer to Leslie or to Cherise. Which choice clarifies "she"?
"One of them" and "the woman" could still refer to either woman, so we should be more specific and say **"Leslie"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Saoirse asked her mother if she had seen __her__ missing sweatshirt.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "Her" could refer to Saoirse or to her mother. Which choice clarifies "her"?
"That one's" and "the woman's" could still refer to either woman, so we should be more specific and say **"Saoirse's"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Ms. Evans and Shira talked about how __she__ would be out of town for a few weeks.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "Her" could refer to Ms. Evans or to Shira. Which choice clarifies "her"?
"The woman" or "one" could still refer to either woman, so we should be more specific and say **"the latter"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>The waiter approached Javier and Hector’s table and asked how __he__ was enjoying the steak.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "He" could refer to Javier or Hector. Which choice clarifies "he"?
"The patron" and "one of them" could still refer to either man, so we should be more specific and say **"Hector"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Marco and his father worked together to turn the old storage room into __his__ office.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "His" could refer to Marco or to his father. Which choice clarifies "his"?
"The man's" and "one's" could still refer to either man, so we should be more specific and say **"Marco's"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>The gorilla and the chimpanzee ran until __one of them__ got tired.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "One of them" could refer to the gorilla or to the chimpanzee. Which choice clarifies "one of them"?
"The animal" and "he" could still refer to either animal, so we should be more specific and say **"the gorilla"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Paula and Angelica rode the train; when an elderly man came aboard, __she__ gave up her seat.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "She" could refer to Paula or to Angelica. Which choice clarifies "she"?
"The woman" and "the rider" could still refer to either woman, so we should be more specific and say **"Paula"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Adriana asked if she could borrow Tara’s camera so Adriana could make a movie about __her.__ 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "One of them" could refer to Adriana or to Tara. Which choice clarifies "one of them"?
"One" and "that girl" could still refer to either girl, so we should be more specific and say **"herself"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Andrea asked Olivia if she could come play chess at __her__ house.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "Her" could refer to Andrea or to her Olivia. Which choice clarifies "her"?
"The girl's" and "a" are still too ambiguous, , so we should be more specific and say **"Olivia's"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Ava told Madison she was glad that __she__ had joined the swim team.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "She" could refer to Ava or to Madison. Which choice clarifies "she"?
"The swimmer" and "one of them" could still refer to either girl, so we should be more specific and say **"Madison"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>On Luna and Maureen’s walk at the beach, __she__ stopped to pick up a mussel shell.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "She" could refer to Luna or to Maureen. Which choice clarifies "she"?
"The explorer" and "the woman" could still refer to either woman, so we should be more specific and say **"Luna"**.

assessment_items.question_content:
**It’s not clear who the __underlined pronoun__ in this sentence is referring to. Which choice makes the sentence clear?**

>Jeanine told Stefania she hoped __she__ would win first prize at the science fair.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: "She" could refer to Jeanine or to Stefania. Which choice clarifies "she"?
"The scientist" and "a student" could still refer to either girl, so we should be more specific and say **"Stefania"**.
